assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a currency different from at least one party's functional currency.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 rather than separate or individual financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable can be considered only if it qualifies as an intragroup monetary item whose exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup dividend
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately permits foreign currency risk on a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction if it is denominated in a non-functional currency and will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 provides a distinct consolidated hedge model when the foreign currency exposure is designated as part of a net investment in a foreign operation.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting in consolidation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The default IFRS 9 rule is that intragroup transactions are not eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless a specific foreign currency exception applies.
